description: High-performance, workflow-driven Shop Management System for automotive repair shops. Manages work orders, jobs, line items, appointments, customers, and vehicles, with real-time notifications, approvals, and sales recovery.

folders.name: config
folders.aiNotes: Use only for database connections/settings. Do not hardcode credentials; rely on .env variables.

folders.name: controllers
folders.aiNotes: Controllers handle business logic only; do not include database schema logic.

folders.name: middleware
folders.role: Request processing, authentication, role validation, error handling, file uploads
folders.aiNotes: Middleware must not include business logic; only auth, validation, error handling, or file processing.

folders.name: models
folders.role: Sequelize data models defining database schema
folders.aiNotes: Models define the schema only; do not include business logic or controllers here.

folders.name: public
folders.aiNotes: Only static assets like CSS, JS, images, and uploads. No server-side logic here.

folders.name: routes
folders.role: Express route definitions mapping endpoints to controllers
folders.aiNotes: Routes map endpoints to controllers; do not include business logic.

folders.name: services
folders.role: Reusable helper modules for API calls, authentication, customer, vehicle, image, settings, VIN handling
folders.aiNotes: Use services for repeated logic, integrations, or WebSocket helpers.

folders.name: views
folders.aiNotes: Views render HTML templates only; do not include business or API logic.

files.name: .env
files.aiNotes: Do not commit secrets; use for DB strings, JWT secrets, etc.

files.name: package.json
files.aiNotes: Use scripts for running, building, testing.

files.name: package-lock.json
files.aiNotes: Do not edit manually; ensures reproducible builds.

files.name: server.js
files.role: Entry point; initializes Express server and Socket.io

files.name: setup.completed.js
files.aiNotes: May contain initialization logic; do not rerun unless instructed.

files.name: README.md
files.aiNotes: Includes overview, folder structure, tech stack, setup instructions, AI guidance.

architecture: Routes map endpoints to controllers only
Middleware handles auth, validation, and file uploads only
Static assets in public folder; no server logic
Services handle reusable helpers and integrations
Maintain folder structure and naming conventions
Do not introduce new frameworks without approval
aiGuidelines: Use README + folder structure as source of truth
Keep controller, model, route responsibilities strict
Prioritize data integrity when generating or modifying code
Read existing files before suggesting changes
Respect naming conventions and coding patterns
Do not modify core files without approval
